title: Suggest Markdown formatting when descriptions and comments are plain text
description:
Kanbus descriptions and comments render Markdown in the console UI. Plain-text submissions are valid but represent a missed opportunity for clarity. When a description or comment contains no Markdown formatting at all, the CLI emits a non-blocking suggestion reminding the caller that Markdown is supported and encouraged.

A "suggestion" is gentler than a warning: it does not imply anything was wrong, it simply surfaces a useful capability at the moment it is most relevant.

Markdown detection heuristic: the text contains none of the following — headings (#), bold/italic (* or _), fenced code blocks (```), inline code (`), blockquotes (>), bullet or numbered lists, horizontal rules, or links ([text](url)).

Scenario: Suggestion emitted when description has no Markdown

Given an agent creates an issue with a plain-text description containing no Markdown syntax
When the CLI creates the issue
Then a suggestion is printed to stderr
And the suggestion notes that Markdown formatting is supported in descriptions
And the suggestion is labelled with a SUGGESTION or HINT prefix distinct from WARNING or ERROR

Scenario: Suggestion emitted when comment has no Markdown

Given an agent adds a plain-text comment with no Markdown syntax
When the CLI adds the comment
Then a suggestion is printed to stderr noting Markdown is supported in comments

Scenario: No suggestion when Markdown is present

Given a description or comment contains at least one Markdown element (e.g. a heading or a code block)
When the CLI processes it
Then no Markdown suggestion is emitted

Scenario: Suggestion also covers update path

Given an agent updates a description or comment with plain-text content
When the CLI processes the update
Then the Markdown suggestion is emitted if no Markdown is detected in the new text

Scenario: Suggestion is concise and token-efficient

Given the suggestion is emitted
Then it is no more than two lines of output
And it provides enough information to act on without further lookup
creator: ryan.porter
